title: Best-server LoRa coverage for fence-line sensors across 4 candidate masts
summary: A 60 km perimeter fence is instrumented with LoRa fence-cut sensors every ~500 m. The reserve has budget for one or two new mast sites in addition to its existing two; this scenario computes which combination of 4 candidate masts gives the best per-sensor coverage. Output is the canonical Op D best-server raster plus the `lora_best_sf` derivative — the field ops lead can see which fence segments fall back to SF12 (slow, robust) and which can run SF7 (fast, cheap on duty cycle).

sites.name: mast-westwall-candidate-A
sites.notes: Candidate; quote in for 25 m self-supporting.

notes: best_server_raster uses 1-indexed values; sidecar JSON maps 1→mast-northgate-existing, 2→mast-southridge-existing, 3→mast-westwall-candidate-A, 4→mast-eastfence-candidate-B. Fence segments at SF12 are the gating constraint — operators considering a 5th mast should focus on those segments. To run with regulatory enforcement for South Africa, attach `regulatory_profile_ref` per the multi-jurisdictional-iot scenario.
